description: A liveBroadcast resource represents an event that will be streamed, via live video, on YouTube.

kind.description: Identifies the API resource's type. Value is youtube#liveBroadcast.

id.description: The ID that YouTube assigns to uniquely identify the broadcast.

snippet.description: Basic details about a live broadcast including its title, description, and scheduled start and end times.

publishedAt.description: The date and time that the broadcast was added to YouTube's live broadcast schedule.

channelId.description: The ID of the channel to which this broadcast is affiliated.

title.description: The broadcast's title. The title is a required field when inserting a broadcast.

description.example: A sample description for this resource.

thumbnails.description: A map of thumbnail images associated with the broadcast.

scheduledStartTime.description: The date and time that the broadcast is scheduled to begin.

scheduledEndTime.description: The date and time that the broadcast is scheduled to end.

actualStartTime.description: The date and time that the broadcast actually started.

actualEndTime.description: The date and time that the broadcast actually ended.

liveChatId.description: The live chat ID associated with the broadcast.

isDefaultBroadcast.description: Indicates whether this broadcast is the default broadcast.

status.description: Status information about a live broadcast including its privacy status and lifecycle status.

lifeCycleStatus.description: The broadcast's status as it relates to the lifecycle of the broadcast.

madeForKids.description: Indicates whether the broadcast is designated as child-directed.

selfDeclaredMadeForKids.description: Indicates whether the channel owner has designated the broadcast as being made for kids.

contentDetails.description: Detailed settings for a live broadcast including stream configuration, DVR settings, and content configuration.

boundStreamId.description: The ID of the stream that is bound to the broadcast.

boundStreamLastUpdateTimeMs.description: The date and time that the live stream referenced by boundStreamId was last updated.

monitorStream.description: The monitorStream object contains information about the monitor stream, which the broadcaster can use to review the event content before the broadcast stream is shown publicly.

enableMonitorStream.description: This value determines whether the monitor stream is enabled for the broadcast.

broadcastStreamDelayMs.description: If you have set the enableMonitorStream property to true, this value sets the delay between the video input and the broadcast monitor stream.

embedHtml.description: The HTML code that embeds a player that plays the monitor stream.

enableEmbed.description: Indicates whether the broadcast video can be played in an embedded player.

enableDvr.description: Indicates whether the broadcast enables DVR controls. DVR controls enable the viewer to control the video playback experience by pausing, rewinding, or fast forwarding portions of the broadcast.

enableContentEncryption.description: Indicates whether YouTube should enable content encryption for the broadcast.

startWithSlate.description: Indicates whether the broadcast should automatically begin with an in-stream slate when you update the broadcast's status to live.

closedCaptionsType.description: Indicates whether the broadcast has captions enabled.

enableLowLatency.description: Indicates whether this broadcast has low latency enabled.

latencyPreference.description: Indicates latency preference for the broadcast. The options are ultraLow, low, and normal.

enableAutoStart.description: This setting indicates whether the broadcast should automatically begin streaming.

enableAutoStop.description: Indicates whether the broadcast should automatically stop streaming.

statistics.description: The statistics object contains info about the live broadcast.

totalChatCount.description: The number of live chat messages currently on the broadcast.

title: LiveBroadcast
